headline: Foxconn second-quarter revenue jumps 40% y/y
summary: Taiwan's Foxconn, the ‌world's largest contract ‌electronics maker and Nvidia's ​biggest server maker, reported on Sunday a jump of ‌39.8% in ⁠second-quarter revenue year on year, ⁠fuelled by strong AI-related demand. Revenue for ​the quarter ​from ​April to ‌June was T$2.513 trillion ($78.71 billion), Foxconn said in a statement, above the LSEG ‌SmartEstimate of ​T$2.372 trillion ​that ​gives greater ‌weight to forecasts from ​analysts ​who are more consistently accurate. ($1 = 31.9260 ​Taiwan ‌dollars)
